title: Pre-requisite: create Neon project + provision DATABASE_URL
description:
Manual step — must be done by the human before the scaffold task runs.

1. Go to https://neon.tech and create a new project (name: arch-btw)
2. Copy the connection string (DATABASE_URL) — pooled endpoint for app, direct endpoint for migrations
3. Add to .env (gitignored):
   DATABASE_URL=<pooled connection string>
   DATABASE_URL_UNPOOLED=<direct connection string>  # used by drizzle-kit migrate
4. Confirm the DB is reachable: psql $DATABASE_URL -c "SELECT 1"
5. Close this issue once .env is populated and the connection is verified

Note: .env.example (committed) should list these key names without values.

title: Update route handlers + types for Excalidraw snapshot format
description: Update src/app/api/debrief/route.ts request type: tlDrawSnapshot -> canvasSnapshot. Update db insert. Update all TypeScript types referencing TLEditorSnapshot to use ExcalidrawElement[].

title: Delete tldraw files and clean up
description: Delete: ComponentShapeUtil.tsx, ComponentIcons.tsx, ComponentPalette.tsx, CanvasClientWrapper.tsx, shapes.ts. Remove tldraw from package.json. Run full quality gates.

title: DB migration: rename tldraw_snapshot to canvas_snapshot
description: ALTER TABLE attempt RENAME COLUMN tldraw_snapshot TO canvas_snapshot. Run via pnpm db:generate + pnpm db:migrate. Verify with Neon MCP.

title: Rewrite serialize.ts for Excalidraw element format
description: Rewrite src/lib/serialize.ts to accept ExcalidrawElement[] instead of TLEditorSnapshot. Nodes: non-arrow elements with customData.componentType. Edges: arrow elements via startBinding.elementId -> endBinding.elementId. Label split on newline for explanation. Full Vitest test coverage.

title: Build Excalidraw library JSON (13 component shapes)
description: Create a .excalidrawlib JSON file with 13 pre-styled component shapes (service, worker, queue, sql-db, nosql-db, cache, object-storage, gateway, third-party, llm-provider, webhook, cron, custom). Each shape uses Excalidraw primitives with roughness:1, hachure fill, component color, customData.componentType, and label. Loaded on canvas mount via updateLibrary.

title: Rewrite DesignCanvas with Excalidraw + library load
description: Replace tldraw Tldraw component with @excalidraw/excalidraw Excalidraw component. Load library on mount. onChange callback captures elements+appState. Notes panel stays as overlay. Remove ComponentShapeUtil, ComponentPalette, ComponentIcons, CanvasClientWrapper. Keep ssr:false dynamic wrapper.

title: Install @excalidraw/excalidraw, remove tldraw
description: Remove tldraw package and install @excalidraw/excalidraw. Update package.json, pnpm-lock.yaml.

title: Debrief: Finish & get verdict -> generateObject scorecard + callbacks + revealed reference + persist
description: Finish & get verdict produces a Zod-typed scorecard via generateObject: per-rubric ratings+comments, one-line verdict (advance/borderline/not yet), 2-3 transcript callbacks, expandable revealed reference (one strong approach). Persist the full attempt row. Acceptance: verdict renders from structured data; attempt saved to DB.
acceptance_criteria:
## Tests (Vitest — required, mock AI SDK and DB)

generateObject / scorecard:
- Happy path: mock generateObject returns a valid Zod scorecard → verdict renders without error
- The returned object satisfies the shared Zod scorecard schema (parse without throw)
- Sad path: generateObject throws → server returns 500, client shows error state (not a blank screen)

Persistence:
- On success, db.insert is called once with all required columns (userId, problemId, snapshotJson, graphJson, transcriptJson, scorecardJson, verdict)
- Sad path: db.insert throws → attempt is not silently lost; error surfaces

Debrief UI:
- Verdict value 'advance' / 'borderline' / 'not yet' each renders the correct label
- Reference panel is collapsed by default, expands on click
- Transcript callbacks (2–3 items) render in the debrief without crash on empty array

## Static checks
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build && pnpm test — all must pass with zero errors

title: Turn-based review loop: Request-review route handler + thread UI + per-turn snapshot
description: Route handler that on Request review serializes current canvas (full snapshot) + optional written reply + prior transcript and streams reviewer comments (streamText) in a London-startup interviewer persona grounded by hidden rubric/reference. Review thread UI beside canvas; user can edit board and reply each turn; re-review repeats. Soft wind-down ~5-7 turns, hard cap ~10. Acceptance: full turn cycle works end to end against a seed problem.
acceptance_criteria:
## Tests (Vitest — required, mock AI SDK and DB)

Route handler — happy paths:
- POST with valid { problemId, snapshot, graphJson, transcript, reply } → streams a response (ReadableStream)
- Turn counter below hard cap (10) → response includes reviewer content
- Turn at soft wind-down (turn 5-7) → response includes a wind-down cue (verify by checking prompt construction, not LLM output)

Route handler — sad paths:
- POST with missing problemId → 400
- POST with malformed JSON body → 400
- Unauthenticated request (no session) → 401
- Turn count >= hard cap → 403 or ends the session cleanly (document the exact behaviour chosen)

Persona / prompt assembly:
- The system prompt includes the hidden rubric and reference from the problem file
- The serialised graph JSON is included in the user turn (verify the prompt string, not the LLM call)

## Static checks
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build && pnpm test — all must pass with zero errors

title: LLM provider layer: AI SDK swappable across Gemini/Anthropic/OpenAI by env key
description: Vercel AI SDK provider abstraction selecting Gemini/Anthropic/OpenAI by whichever of GEMINI_API_KEY/ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/OPENAI_API_KEY is present. Server-side only. Helpers for streamText and generateObject. Acceptance: switching env key switches provider; browser never receives a key.
acceptance_criteria:
## Tests (Vitest — required)

Happy paths:
- GEMINI_API_KEY set → getProvider() returns a Gemini model instance
- ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set → returns an Anthropic model instance
- OPENAI_API_KEY set → returns an OpenAI model instance
- streamText helper called with a provider → returns a ReadableStream (mock AI SDK)
- generateObject helper called with a schema → resolves to typed object (mock AI SDK)

Sad/edge paths:
- No API key set → getProvider() throws a clear error (not a silent undefined)
- Multiple keys set → picks in priority order (Gemini > Anthropic > OpenAI) without crash
- API key present but empty string → treated as not set

Security:
- Confirm provider module is server-only: add `import 'server-only'` at top; verify build does not bundle it client-side (check next build output for the file)

## Static checks
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build && pnpm test — all must pass with zero errors

title: Canvas: tldraw integration with 12 custom component shapes + explanations + notes + directed arrows
description: Integrate tldraw. Custom ShapeUtil per component type (Service, Worker, Queue/Stream, SQL DB, NoSQL, Cache, Object storage, Gateway/LB, Third-party API, LLM provider, Webhook source, Cron, Custom). Each shape has an optional explanation prop. Drag-from-palette, directed arrows, global train-of-thought notes panel. Acceptance: can drag, connect with directed arrows, edit per-box explanation + notes.

title: Serialization adapter: tldraw store -> {nodes,edges,notes} JSON (tested)
description: First-class, unit-tested adapter converting the tldraw store (custom shapes + arrow bindings) into clean directed { nodes, edges, notes } JSON for the LLM. Critique quality depends on this. Acceptance: tests cover topology, direction, explanations, notes; round-trips representative boards.
acceptance_criteria:
## Tests (Vitest — required)

Happy paths:
- Single node, no edges → { nodes: [node], edges: [], notes: '' }
- Two nodes connected by directed arrow A→B → edges contains { from: A, to: B }
- Node with explanation filled → node.explanation equals the value
- Notes panel content → notes string in output

Sad/edge paths:
- Empty store (no shapes) → { nodes: [], edges: [], notes: '' }
- Arrow with no source binding → excluded from edges (no crash)
- Arrow with no target binding → excluded from edges (no crash)
- Unknown shape type → included in nodes as type: 'unknown' or similar (no throw)
- Bidirectional arrows (A→B and B→A) → two separate edge entries

Round-trip: serialise a board, verify the JSON re-imports to an equivalent store without data loss.

## Static checks
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build && pnpm test — all must pass with zero errors

title: Data model: Drizzle attempts schema + Zod scorecard + problem types + 1 seed problem
description: Drizzle schema for attempts (userId, problemId, tldraw snapshot JSON, graph JSON, transcript JSON, scorecard JSON, verdict, createdAt). Zod scorecard schema shared by AI SDK/DB/UI. Problem TS type. One sample static server-only seed problem. Acceptance: migration applies, types compile, sample problem imports server-side only.
acceptance_criteria:
## Tests (Vitest — required)

Zod scorecard schema:
- Happy path: valid scorecard object satisfies the schema without errors
- Sad path: missing required field (e.g. verdict) → parse throws ZodError
- Sad path: verdict value outside enum → ZodError

Problem type:
- A well-formed seed problem object satisfies the Problem type at compile time
- Server-only import: the seed problem file must not be importable from a client component — verify with a `import 'server-only'` guard

Drizzle schema / migrations:
- Run `pnpm db:generate` → produces a migration file without errors
- Verify the migration applied correctly using the Neon MCP tool (`run_sql` with a `SELECT` against the new table) — no manual psql needed

## Static checks
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build && pnpm test — all must pass with zero errors

title: Auth: Better Auth + GitHub OAuth + session
description: Better Auth with GitHub OAuth via the Drizzle adapter. Sessions, users table managed by Better Auth. Gives a userId to hang attempts off. Acceptance: sign in with GitHub, session persists, userId available server-side.
acceptance_criteria:
## Tests (Vitest — mock Better Auth session)

Happy paths:
- Authenticated request: mock session returns a userId → server action/route can access it
- Session object shape matches what downstream routes expect (userId is a non-empty string)

Sad paths:
- Unauthenticated request: getSession() returns null → protected route returns 401

Manual smoke (document in progress.md):
- Sign in with GitHub → redirected back, session cookie set, userId visible in server log

## Static checks
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build && pnpm test — all must pass with zero errors

title: [Epic] arch-btw v1: system-design interview canvas
description: Single-player portfolio + learning tool. Drag prefab components onto a tldraw canvas, connect with directed arrows, explain each box, then defend the design in a turn-based PR-review loop with an LLM interviewer (London-startup style, not FAANG). Ends in a structured scorecard debrief. See mempalace wing arch-btw/decisions for full design.

title: Project scaffold: Next.js App Router + TS strict + Tailwind/shadcn + Drizzle + Neon
description:
Bootstrap the app: Next.js App Router (latest stable, React 19), TypeScript strict, Tailwind + shadcn/ui, Drizzle ORM wired to Neon Postgres, env config (.env with LLM keys, GitHub OAuth, Neon URL, Better Auth secret).

Also set up the test infrastructure:
- Install vitest, @vitest/ui, jsdom, @testing-library/react, @testing-library/jest-dom as devDeps
- Create vitest.config.ts (jsdom environment, path aliases matching tsconfig)
- Add pnpm scripts: test (vitest run), test:watch (vitest), test:ui (vitest --ui)
- Write one smoke test (src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts) that asserts 1 + 1 === 2 — proves the runner works
- Ensure pnpm test passes as part of CI / quality gates

Acceptance: app builds, connects to Neon, shadcn component renders, pnpm test passes.
acceptance_criteria:
## Checks (no unit tests needed for a scaffold — static checks + smoke test)

- pnpm typecheck → zero errors
- pnpm lint → zero warnings
- pnpm build → succeeds (no missing env vars blocking the build)
- pnpm dev starts without crash
- Smoke: navigating to / renders a shadcn Button component (proves Tailwind + shadcn wired)
- Smoke: Drizzle client connects to Neon without error (a simple db.execute('SELECT 1') in a test route or script)
- .env.example committed with all required keys listed (no secrets, just key names)

## Static checks
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build — all must pass with zero errors

title: Author 6 curated seed problems (2 easy / 2 medium / 2 hard)
description: LLM-draft then human-curate 6 feature/integration/AI-agent flavored problems, each with prompt, canned clarification facts, hidden rubric, hidden reference. Stored as static server-only typed files. Acceptance: 6 problems validate against the problem schema and load server-side.

title: Deploy to Vercel + env/secrets wiring
description: Deploy to Vercel with Neon, GitHub OAuth, Better Auth secret, and the chosen LLM key configured. Verify streaming review turns work on Vercel. Acceptance: production deploy completes a full review loop + debrief.
close_reason: vercel.json + env wiring done; live deploy requires secrets (BETTER_AUTH_SECRET, GITHUB_CLIENT_ID/SECRET, LLM key) set in Vercel project settings

title: Progress view: list past attempts and scores over time
description: Authenticated view listing the user past attempts per problem with verdicts and rubric scores over time. Acceptance: attempts list renders for the signed-in user.

title: Clarification beat: optional pre-design Q&A from canned facts
description: Before/while designing, user can ask clarifying questions; interviewer answers consistently from the problem canned clarification facts. Acceptance: asking a clarifying question returns an in-character answer grounded only in canned facts.
